sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, a 30-year-old male, presents with a one-week history of neck pain, which he attributes to a car accident.
He describes the pain as a 7/10, which worsens with neck movement.
The pain is localized to the back of his neck, and he reports that it has not improved with ibuprofen.
He has had occasional headaches, but no visual disturbances or hearing problems.
He denies any prior neck injuries or sports-related trauma.
Past medical history: [NOT MENTIONED]
Review of systems: [NOT MENTIONED]
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient exhibited positive pain to palpation of the soft tissues of the neck, with worsening pain on extension, flexion, and rotation.
He was able to turn his head from side to side, but with pain.
Test Results: X-ray results show no signs of fracture or bony abnormalities.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has neck strain, likely due to whiplash from the car accident.
Problem list: Neck strain; suspected whiplash; unknown
Plan:
I prescribed Motrin 600mg every 6-8 hours for inflammation, and Flexeril 10mg every 12 hours as needed for muscle relaxation.
I advised the patient to avoid straining his neck and to be conservative with his movements.
I ordered an MRI to rule out any nerve impingement or other complications.
I plan to refer the patient to either physical rehab or a chiropractor once the MRI results are available.
Follow-up: The patient will be followed up with the results of the MRI and to assess his progress.
